claim: Function Gemma (270M parameters) out-of-the-box achieves 46% accuracy on app intents; fine-tuned synthetically on custom functions, it reaches 90%+ accuracy—proving tiny models can be reliable with proper training.
actionable: Don't reject 270M models based on raw performance; fine-tune them on your specific tasks (function calls, intents) using synthetic data to reach production quality.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: Tiny LLMs (< 1B params) split into two deployment modes: Gemini Nano via AI Core (system-level, pre-installed on device, free) or LiteRT-LM for custom boutique models (app-bundled, full customization).
actionable: Choose Gemini Nano for common tasks (summarization, Q&A); use LiteRT-LM for custom tasks requiring fine-tuning (restaurant roulette, domain-specific intent detection).
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: On-device execution (Pixel 7) with Function Gemma achieves 2,000 tokens/sec prefill and 140 tokens/sec decode—sufficient for real-time voice-to-function-calling without server latency.
actionable: Deploy Function Gemma for voice agents and real-time intent detection; acceptable latency for on-device execution removes server dependency and privacy concerns.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: Agent skill harness on Gemma 4 works via system prompts, skill descriptions (loaded on demand), and tool calls (load_skill, show_JS)—enabling JavaScript UIs directly in the app.
actionable: Build skills that include lightweight JavaScript for UI rendering; use load_skill and show_JS tool calls to selectively load only needed skills and show custom interfaces.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: Use larger models (like Gemini) with natural language to synthetically generate fine-tuning datasets for tiny LLMs—Flash generates data, you train the tiny model, production uses the small model.
actionable: Use Gemini's Function Gemma fine-tuning lab (HuggingFace space): define functions, have Flash generate synthetic examples, upload to fine-tune Function Gemma for your domain.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: LiteRT runtime is open-source, works on CPU/GPU/NPU, and is already embedded in Android OS (2.7B device version)—making tiny model deployment trusted and scalable.
actionable: Use LiteRT for production on-device deployment; it's battle-tested at scale and supports hardware acceleration via NPU when available.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: Eloquent (iOS transcription app) chains two tiny Gemma 3 models (ASR + text polishing, each a few 100M params) to provide offline transcription with personal dictionary support—proof tiny models work in production.
actionable: Model chaining works for complex tasks: use separate tiny models for sub-tasks (transcription → polishing) and chain them to achieve quality comparable to monolithic larger models.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: Don't assume smaller models are unreliable—real-world benchmarks on deep agents show open models (Qwen 3.5, Gemma 4) approaching frontier model performance; fine-tuning for specific domains closes the gap further.
actionable: Benchmark smaller models on YOUR tasks before dismissing them; they often exceed expectations with task-specific fine-tuning.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google

claim: Gemini CLI and tools like ADB skill-writing showcase that agents can create and test skills autonomously—you can ask Claude to write your agent skills, validate via ADB, then use them.
actionable: Use Gemini to programmatically generate skills; leverage ADB/debug tools for validation; republish as community skills for others to use.
source_title: From 46% to 90%: Fine-Tuning Tiny LLMs for On-Device Agents — Cormac Brick, Google
